paper_notes: Optimization is sequential rather than formal DOE. Variable exploration and optimization are distributed across tables and result paragraphs.

PREPARATION_METHOD.selection_intent: Frontload the base nanoprecipitation procedure reused across the sequential optimization steps.

expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: synthesized by nanoprecipitation... dissolving PLGA in acetone... added to aqueous phase... evaporation... filtered... centrifuged

PREPARATION_METHOD.inclusion_notes: This should stay near the top because all optimization steps reuse the same base process.
PREPARATION_METHOD.exclusion_notes: Do not replace with commercial comparator descriptions.
PREPARATION_METHOD.notes: No numeric subsection labels, so lexical procedural cues dominate.

MATERIALS.selection_intent: Frontload the core formulation components and relevant comparators.

expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: PLGA... poloxamer 188... ITZ... solvents... purchased from Sigma-Aldrich

MATERIALS.inclusion_notes: This should appear early, but comparator-only material lists can stay lower priority.
MATERIALS.exclusion_notes: Do not let problem-background sentences dominate this role.
MATERIALS.notes: Clean inventory with some comparator context.

EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN.selection_intent: Retain evidence that optimization proceeds through two sequential variable screens even without formal DOE language.

expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: PLGA concentration was kept constant... four different poloxamer 188 concentrations were used
various amounts of ITZ were dissolved... to obtain PLGA:ITZ ratios of 5:1, 10:1, and 15:1

EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN.inclusion_notes: Helpful, but less important than the actual tables and optimization result paragraphs.
EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN.exclusion_notes: Do not force a formal DOE interpretation.

VARIABLE_TABLE.selection_intent: Frontload the varying conditions explored across the two optimization steps.

expected_sources.section_title: Physicochemical properties of unloaded PLGA nanoparticles stabilized by different concentrations of poloxamer 188
Physicochemical properties of PLGA-ITZ-NS with different PLGA:ITZ initial ratios

VARIABLE_TABLE.inclusion_notes: Needed because variable definitions are embedded inside result tables.
VARIABLE_TABLE.exclusion_notes: Do not expect pure factor-only tables.
VARIABLE_TABLE.notes: Variable and outcome information are merged.

FORMULATION_TABLE.selection_intent: Frontload measured outcomes across the screened formulations.

expected_sources.section_title: Physicochemical properties of unloaded PLGA nanoparticles stabilized by different concentrations of poloxamer 188
expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: particle size, PDI, zeta potential across different concentrations

expected_sources.section_title: Physicochemical properties of PLGA-ITZ-NS with different PLGA:ITZ initial ratios
expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: particle size, PDI, zeta potential, EE across different PLGA:ITZ ratios

FORMULATION_TABLE.inclusion_notes: This is core evidence for sequential optimization reasoning.
FORMULATION_TABLE.exclusion_notes: Do not split away from variable context if tables are inherently fused.
FORMULATION_TABLE.notes: Outcome tables also carry variable context.

OPTIMIZATION_RESULT.selection_intent: Frontload the two optimization conclusions that identify the final working conditions.

expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: 3 mg/mL of poloxamer 188 was chosen for the preparation of PLGA-ITZ-NS during the whole study
PLGA:ITZ prepared with an initial ratio of 10:1 was chosen as the optimal formulation

OPTIMIZATION_RESULT.inclusion_notes: This should be retained in full because optimization closure is split across more than one paragraph.
OPTIMIZATION_RESULT.exclusion_notes: Do not keep only one of the two optimization decisions.

CONTEXT_FALLBACK.selection_intent: Retain limited supporting narrative only when it helps connect the variable screens to the final selected formulation.

expected_sources.raw_text_excerpt: narrative comparison of screened conditions leading into the selected PLGA:ITZ formulation

CONTEXT_FALLBACK.inclusion_notes: Fallback context should remain secondary to variable tables, formulation tables, and explicit optimization conclusions.
CONTEXT_FALLBACK.exclusion_notes: Do not use fallback to replace missing primary optimization evidence.
CONTEXT_FALLBACK.notes: Supporting context is useful because the optimization story is distributed rather than expressed in one closed section.
